en: This requirement is triggered because you indicated your organization maintains valid Employer Payroll Tax Registration documentation in the Germany Employer Payroll Tax Registration Applicability Assessment questionnaire.
Germany Employer Payroll Tax Registration Transmission Protocol

Employers in Germany must withhold wage tax from employees’ wages and, after electronic submission of the Employer Payroll Tax Registration to the competent tax office, pay this tax to that office. This requirement is laid out in the § 41a Income Tax Act (EStG) and the § 150 Tax Code (AO).\n\nThe Transmission Protocol can be obtained directly inside the company’s Mein ELSTER account at the time of, and after, submitting the Employer Payroll Tax Registration.
Please upload your Transmission Protocol document to confirm your successful submission of the Employer Payroll Tax Registration.
Please enter the issue date of your Transmission Protocol document.

The expiration date of your Transmission Protocol document will depend on your Employer Payroll Tax Registration filing period (monthly, quarterly, yearly). § 41a EStG and the official guidance state that the filing period depends on the wage tax due in the previous calendar year. Monthly filers would indicate an expiration date as end of each calendar month. Quarterly filers would indicate an expiration date as end of each calendar quarter. Annual filers would indicate an expiration date as end of the calendar year.
Please enter the expiration date of your Transmission Protocol document.
